session_summary: Fixed all 8 warning-level code review issues (WR-01 to WR-08); completed 4 of 5 critical issues

completed_tasks.name: SQL Injection Risk

completed_tasks.name: Analytics N+1 Query Optimization

human_actions_pending.context: Verify all 13 updated endpoints work correctly and no regressions introduced
Team review of 2 commits before merging to main
Schedule server-side session management for auth bypass fix

commits.message: fix(phase-05): fix database connection leaks and add input validation
fix(phase-05): optimize N+1 query in patient statistics endpoint

decisions.decision: Use DBContext pattern for database connections
decisions.rationale: Context manager guarantees cleanup in all code paths (success, exception, early return); eliminates resource leaks

decisions.decision: Replace N+1 query with GROUP BY aggregate
decisions.rationale: Reduces database round-trips from O(n) to O(1); critical performance improvement for clinics with large patient counts
Client-side auth bypass requires full architectural redesign (hardcoded IDs → server-side sessions); properly scoped for Phase 6

next_action: Run full test suite; if passing, merge both commits to main; create Phase 6 plan for CR-01
testing_status: All code compiles successfully; full integration test suite not yet run

infrastructure_state.database: Using existing clinic.db; schema already updated for 'staff' sender

context_notes: Session focused on defensive programming patterns: resource leaks (DBContext), input validation, query optimization, SQL injection prevention. All warning-level issues resolved. Codebase is now more robust and ready for Phase 6 (server-side auth implementation).
